articles.title: Peace Talks on the Move: U.S. and Iran Head to Pakistan
articles.summary: Have you ever seen two kids argue over a toy and then try to make up? That's kind of what's happening between the U.S. and Iran, but with way bigger stakes. After two months of war, both sides are sending people to Pakistan to talk about peace. The big issue is the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway where ships carry oil. Right now, it's mostly blocked, which makes gas prices high. The U.S. is sending special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Iran's foreign minister is already in Pakistan, but says he won't meet with the Americans yet. Still, the White House says Iran wants to talk. A ceasefire has been holding for three weeks. Will these talks finally…

articles.title: A Carousel with a Story: Smithsonian's Ride Reopens After 3 Years
articles.summary: After being closed for nearly three years, the Smithsonian carousel on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., is spinning again! But this isn't just any merry-go-round. It has an amazing history. Back in the 1960s, it was at an amusement park in Maryland that only allowed white people. Brave kids and adults protested until everyone could ride. On August 28, 1963 — the same day Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his famous "I Have a Dream" speech — the park was finally open to all. Now, the carousel has 54 horses, a sea monster, a pig, and two chariots. Kids love it. One 7-year-old said it's one of the fastest carousels he's ever been on!

articles.title: Alcaraz Out of French Open: Wrist Injury Dashes Threepeat Dream
articles.summary: Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the 22-year-old Spanish superstar who won the French Open the last two years, won't be playing this year. He hurt his right wrist during a match in Barcelona and has to sit out the tournament. Alcaraz said on social media that he and his team decided it's best to be careful and not risk making the injury worse. He also won't play in the Italian Open. Alcaraz just won the Australian Open in January, becoming the youngest man to win all four Grand Slams. His rival Jannik Sinner, who beat Alcaraz last month, said it's sad news and hopes Alcaraz will be back for Wimbledon in July.
